summary: Phase 7 (Smart appointment reminder + .ics endpoint) backend tests: 13/13 pass. Verified HMAC-protected /calendar.ics endpoint (200/403/404/410), dynamic cancel/reschedule windows, reschedule_remaining math, push notification creation, admin trigger endpoint, and email body/HTML content. One important behavioral issue with Resend configuration was found and surfaced.

minor.issue: If services.email.send_appointment_reminder raises (e.g. real Resend API key set but domain unverified -> EmailServiceError), the outer try/except swallows the exception BEFORE the push notification is created and BEFORE 'reminder_sent' flag is set. Effect: (a) in-app booking_reminder push never fires when email provider fails, (b) the same booking is retried every 30 min forever. Recommendation: wrap the `await send_appointment_reminder(...)` call in its own try/except so push + reminder_sent still execute on email failure.

RESEND_API_KEY is set to a real key but the sender domain (hola@bookvia.app) returns 'The associated domain with your API key is not verified'. As a result, every reminder email currently fails. Either remove RESEND_API_KEY (so is_resend_configured()=False -> mock path stores in db.sent_emails) OR verify the domain in Resend. Test had to monkeypatch is_resend_configured() to False to validate happy-path.

action_items: Wrap send_appointment_reminder call in a try/except inside send_appointment_reminders() so email failure does not block push notification creation and reminder_sent flag update.
Either verify the bookvia.app domain in Resend or unset RESEND_API_KEY in backend/.env to keep email service in mock mode for staging/preview.
critical_code_review_comments: Calendar .ics endpoint correctly uses HMAC-SHA256 with JWT_SECRET (32-char hex truncation), validates with hmac.compare_digest. Returns 403 for missing/invalid token, 404 for unknown booking, 410 for cancelled/expired/no_show. Output is RFC5545-compliant including BEGIN/END VCALENDAR/VEVENT/VALARM, TRIGGER:-PT2H, business name in SUMMARY, booking_id in UID. No issues.

send_appointment_reminders() correctly computes cancel_free_until (24h before) and reschedule_until (RESCHEDULE_CUTOFF_HOURS=2 before), forces reschedule_remaining=0 when reschedule cutoff passed, and respects MAX_RESCHEDULES_PER_BOOKING=2. Spanish month abbreviations (_MONTHS) work for date formatting.
Push notification (create_notification) is created with type='booking_reminder' and the expected data payload, but only when the email path does NOT raise. See minor issue above.
POST /api/bookings/send-reminders requires admin auth; unauthenticated calls return 403, admin-token calls return 200 + {message}.
Email HTML correctly includes 3 action URLs (action=cancel, action=reschedule, calendar.ics?token=) and Spanish copy ('Cancelacion con reembolso', 'Reagendar gratis').
test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md admin (zamorachapa50@gmail.com) with TOTP from db.users.totp_secret via pyotp. Test user/booking/service seeded directly in MongoDB with TEST_F7_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Seeded test user user_test_fase7 (TEST_F7_user@test.com), service TEST_F7_SVC under existing biz_dd14ed093b51, and bookings prefixed TEST_F7_BK_*. All cleaned up in teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_fase7_smart_reminder.py monkeypatches services.email.is_resend_configured to return False so emails go through the mock path (Resend domain is unverified in this preview env). The test fixture _create_booking formats date/time in America/Mexico_City to align with the scheduler's tz interpretation. Tests use a session-scoped event_loop fixture so Motor (async mongo client) reuses the same loop across all async invocations.
rca of the issue: N/A - all tests passed. Behavioral concern documented in backend_issues.minor: email-failure path skips push + flag update; combined with currently-failing Resend domain, every reminder is currently silently failing in production-like envs and being retried every 30 min.
